Long ago, before the great mountains even had names, the deep valleys of the world were completely silent. The wind blew fiercely, but it made no sound. In these quiet lands lived Aethel, a curious spirit of the winds who desperately craved music. She would often fly to the edge of the world just to listen to the gods sing, but she could never bring their beautiful melodies back with her.

One night, driven by an insatiable longing, Aethel wove a delicate net out of starlight and cast it over the sleeping gods, catching their divine voices. She fled back to the mortal realm and unleashed the stolen sounds. Suddenly, the rushing rivers roared, the autumn leaves rustled, and the canyon stones hummed with life. 

But the gods awoke in absolute silence and were furious. The Sun God tracked Aethel down, finding her cowering in a deep, rocky ravine. As punishment for her great theft, he bound her spirit to the cold stone. He decreed that she could never create her own music again. Instead, she was cursed to instantly throw back any sound that reached her. 

To this day, Aethel creates the Echo.
